title: Pod Creation and Management
description: Create and manage pods with various configurations including resource limits and environment variables
context: You need to create and manage pods with specific configurations for a production environment.

tasks.title: Create a basic pod
tasks.description: Create a pod named 'web-server' using nginx:1.20 image in the default namespace

commands: kubectl get pod web-server --no-headers | grep Running

hints: Use 'kubectl run' command or create a YAML manifest
Make sure the pod name is exactly 'web-server'

tasks.title: Add resource limits
tasks.description: Modify the pod to include resource limits: CPU 100m, Memory 128Mi
hints: Edit the pod YAML to add resources.limits section
Use 'kubectl edit pod web-server' or apply a new manifest
CPU should be '100m' and memory should be '128Mi'

tasks.title: Add environment variable
tasks.description: Add environment variable APP_ENV=production to the pod
hints: Add env section to the container specification

tasks.title: Verify pod logs
tasks.description: Check that the pod is running correctly and nginx is serving requests

commands: kubectl logs web-server | grep 'start worker process'

hints: Use 'kubectl logs web-server' to check pod logs
Nginx should show worker processes starting
If no logs appear, the pod might not be running correctly

resources.name: pod-template
resources.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: web-server
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
    image: nginx:1.20
    # Add your resource limits and environment variables here
